 # Graphite — Project Specification & Build Guide
 
 ## What is Graphite?
 
 Graphite is an elegant markdown writing IDE built with Electron. It's a four-panel writing environment designed for writers who care about craft. Think Linear meets Obsidian — refined, quiet, precise.
 
 The name comes from the material pencils are made of. The design language follows that metaphor: cool grays, refined typography, and an interface that feels like opening a beautiful notebook.
 
 ---
 
 ## Architecture Overview
 
 ```
 graphite/
 ├── package.json
 ├── electron/
 │   ├── main.js              # Electron main process
 │   ├── preload.js            # Preload script (context bridge)
 │   └── ipc-handlers.js       # IPC handlers for file system, etc.
 ├── src/
 │   ├── index.html            # Entry HTML
 │   ├── main.jsx              # React entry point
 │   ├── App.jsx               # Root app component
 │   ├── styles/
 │   │   ├── globals.css       # CSS variable declarations, resets, scrollbar styles
 │   │   ├── typography.css    # Preview typography (Source Serif 4)
 │   │   └── animations.css    # Keyframes and transitions
 │   ├── themes/
 │   │   ├── theme-loader.js   # Reads themes, applies CSS variables to :root
 │   │   ├── theme-registry.js # Discovers built-in + user-installed themes
 │   │   └── built-in/
 │   │       ├── silver.json   # Default standalone (dark)
 │   │       ├── slate.json    # Dark half of "ink" pair
 │   │       ├── frost.json    # Light half of "ink" pair
 │   │       ├── ivory.json    # Dark half of "paper" pair
 │   │       └── paper.json    # Light half of "paper" pair
 │   ├── components/
 │   │   ├── TitleBar.jsx      # App title bar with panel toggles
 │   │   ├── StatusBar.jsx     # Bottom bar: word count, char count, read time
 │   │   ├── ThemeSwitcher.jsx # Theme picker UI (in settings or title bar)
 │   │   ├── FileExplorer/
 │   │   │   ├── FileExplorer.jsx
 │   │   │   ├── FileNode.jsx
 │   │   │   └── FileSearch.jsx
 │   │   ├── Editor/
 │   │   │   ├── Editor.jsx           # CodeMirror 6 wrapper
 │   │   │   └── editor-theme.js      # Custom CM6 theme (reads CSS variables)
 │   │   ├── Preview/
 │   │   │   ├── Preview.jsx          # Rendered markdown preview
 │   │   │   └── markdown-parser.js   # Markdown-it or remark pipeline
 │   │   └── AIChat/
 │   │       ├── AIChat.jsx           # Chat panel container
 │   │       ├── ChatMessage.jsx      # Individual message component
 │   │       ├── ChatInput.jsx        # Input with send button
 │   │       └── ContextDrawer.jsx    # Pinned sources/context for AI
 │   ├── hooks/
 │   │   ├── useFileSystem.js   # File read/write/watch via IPC
 │   │   ├── useMarkdown.js     # Markdown parsing hook
 │   │   ├── useTheme.js        # Theme state, toggle, persistence
 │   │   └── useAI.js           # AI chat hook (GitHub Copilot SDK)
 │   ├── lib/
 │   │   ├── ai-client.js      # GitHub Copilot SDK integration
 │   │   ├── spell-check.js    # Spell/grammar checking
 │   │   └── file-utils.js     # Path helpers, file type detection
 │   └── icons/
 │       └── Icons.jsx          # All SVG icons as React components
 ├── resources/
 │   └── icon.png              # App icon (hexagonal graphite mark)
 ├── CLAUDE.md                 # Claude Code configuration
 └── README.md
 ```
 
 ---
 
 ## Tech Stack
 
 | Layer | Choice | Why |
 |-------|--------|-----|
 | Shell | Electron | Desktop app, full filesystem access, Node.js backend |
 | Frontend | React 18 | Component model, hooks, ecosystem |
 | Bundler | Vite | Fast HMR, good Electron support via electron-vite |
 | Editor | CodeMirror 6 | Purpose-built for text editing, extensible, performant |
 | Markdown | markdown-it | Fast, pluggable, good defaults |
 | AI | GitHub Copilot SDK | Programmatic AI access (chat completions) |
 | Spell Check | Electron built-in + hunspell | Native spell check support |
 | Packaging | electron-builder | Cross-platform builds |
 
 ---
 
 ## Design System
 
 ### Theme Architecture
 
 Graphite ships with 5 themes organized into **one standalone + two pairs**:
 
 - **Silver** (standalone) — the default. Literally the color of graphite. The brand theme.
 - **Slate & Frost** (paired) — the "ink" pair. Cool, intellectual, blue-steel DNA.
 - **Ivory & Paper** (paired) — the "paper" pair. Warm-neutral, gentle, material-focused.
 
 Users choose a theme group, and Graphite automatically uses the correct dark/light variant based on system preference or manual toggle. Silver uses its own single palette for both (it's dark-only by default; if the user toggles light mode while on Silver, it falls back to Frost).

result.content:
1. # Graphite — Claude Code Configuration
2. 
3. ## Project Overview
4. Graphite is an Electron-based markdown writing IDE with a flexible panel system: file explorer, editor (CodeMirror 6), live preview, and AI writing companion. It features a theme system with paired dark/light themes and movable/resizable panels.
5. 
6. ## Tech Stack
7. - Electron + electron-vite
8. - React 18
9. - CodeMirror 6 (editor)
10. - markdown-it (preview rendering)
11. - GitHub Copilot SDK (@github/copilot-sdk) — AI chat with streaming
12. - JSON-based theme system
13. 
14. ## Design Principles
15. - This is a WRITING tool, not a code editor. Every decision should favor the writing experience.
16. - The aesthetic is "Linear meets Obsidian" — refined, quiet, precise.
17. - The default theme is "Silver" (cool graphite, silvery accent #8b9bb5).
18. - Typography: DM Sans for UI, Source Serif 4 for preview, JetBrains Mono for editor.
19. - Motion should be subtle and purposeful. No decorative animations.
20. - The app should feel like opening a beautiful notebook.
21. - ALL colors must come from CSS variables set by the theme loader. Never hardcode colors.
22. 
23. ## Panel System Architecture
24. - Panels are self-contained, position-agnostic components
25. - User can drag panels to reorder (left ↔ right)
26. - User can resize panel widths via drag handles
27. - Panel positions/sizes persist to user preferences
28. - Terminal panel type is reserved for v2 (scaffolded but not implemented)
29. 
30. ## Theme System
31. - Themes are JSON files defining CSS variable values (see src/themes/built-in/)
32. - 5 built-in themes: Silver, Slate, Ivory (dark) and Frost, Paper (light)
33. - Single dropdown shows all themes grouped by Dark/Light category
34. - Custom themes can be installed by dropping JSON into ~/.graphite/themes/
35. - The CodeMirror theme in editor-theme.js MUST read from CSS variables, not hardcoded values
36. - When adding any new UI element, always use CSS variables (--bg-primary, --accent, etc.)
37. 
38. ## Code Style
39. - Functional React components with hooks
40. - CSS variables for ALL design tokens (set by theme loader at runtime)
41. - No CSS-in-JS libraries — use CSS modules or plain CSS
42. - File naming: PascalCase for components, camelCase for utilities
43. - Keep components focused and small (< 150 lines ideally)
44. 
45. ## Key Files
46. - `electron/main.js` — Main process, window creation, IPC setup, AI handlers
47. - `electron/preload.js` — Context bridge for renderer (includes theme IPC, menu events, AI API)
48. - `electron/ai-client.js` — Copilot SDK wrapper, session management, streaming, custom tools
49. - `src/App.jsx` — Root component, file loading, dirty state, save handling
50. - `src/lib/command-registry.js` — Slash command definitions for AI chat
51. - `src/lib/diff-utils.js` — Line-based LCS diff algorithm for AI edit previews
52. - `src/themes/theme-loader.js` — Reads JSON themes, applies CSS variables to :root
53. - `src/themes/theme-registry.js` — Discovers built-in + user themes, manages pairs
54. - `src/hooks/useTheme.js` — Theme state, persistence
55. - `src/hooks/useFileSystem.js` — File operations (read, write, directory listing)
56. - `src/hooks/useAISettings.js` — AI settings hook (auto-apply, diff preview, reasoning effort)
57. - `src/components/Editor/editor-theme.js` — CodeMirror theme (MUST use CSS variables)
58. - `src/components/FileExplorer/FileNode.jsx` — Tree item with expand/collapse
59. - `src/components/FileExplorer/FileSearch.jsx` — Search filtering
60. - `src/components/ThemeSwitcher/ThemeSwitcher.jsx` — Theme dropdown (Dark/Light groups)
61. - `src/components/Panels/PanelManager.jsx` — Context provider for panel state
62. - `src/components/Panels/PanelContainer.jsx` — Resizable, reorderable layout
63. - `src/components/ActivityRail/ActivityRail.jsx` — Left navigation rail
64. - `src/components/ActivityRail/RailButton.jsx` — Individual rail icon button
65. - `src/components/Settings/SettingsModal.jsx` — Settings dialog (includes Writing Companion section)
66. - `src/components/AIChat/AIChat.jsx` — AI chat panel with streaming, diff preview, user input prompts
67. - `src/components/AIChat/DiffView.jsx` — Unified diff preview component with Accept/Reject
68. - `src/components/AIChat/DiffView.css` — Diff styling (uses theme semantic variables)
69. - `src/styles/globals.css` — CSS variable declarations and resets
70. 
71. ## Testing
72. 
73. ### Automated Tests
74. - `npm run test` — builds then runs all Playwright tests
75. - `npx playwright test --project=functional` — runs functional tests only (requires prior build)
76. - `npx playwright test --project=visual` — runs visual/layout tests only
77. - `node tests/diff-utils.test.js` — runs diff algorithm unit tests (standalone Node)
78. - `node tests/css-audit.test.js` — scans all CSS files for hardcoded color violations
79. 
80. ### Test Files
81. - `tests/app.spec.js` — basic app launch and UI element verification
82. - `tests/functional.spec.js` — core workflow: file open, edit, save, panel toggles, theme switch
83. - `tests/selection-toolbar.spec.js` — selection toolbar appear/disappear
84. - `tests/feedback-modal.spec.js` — feedback modal open/close, validation states
85. - `tests/settings-modal.spec.js` — settings modal, Writing Companion section (toggles, dropdown, persistence)
86. - `tests/diff-utils.test.js` — unit tests for `computeLineDiff` and `diffSummary`
87. - `tests/css-audit.test.js` — CSS variable discipline audit (flags hardcoded hex/rgb colors)
88. - `tests/visual.spec.js`, `tests/layout-*.spec.js`, `tests/theme-parity.spec.js` — visual regression tests
89. 
90. ### Manual QA Checklist
91. - The editor should feel responsive with no lag on keystroke
92. - Preview should update live as you type
93. - File explorer should reflect actual filesystem
94. - Switching themes should be instant (no flash, no reload)
95. - All 5 built-in themes should look correct
96. - Panel reordering and resizing should be smooth
97. - Dirty indicator (●) should appear when file is modified
98. 
99. ## Important Notes
100. - No line numbers in the editor
101. - Soft word wrap enabled
102. - List items in preview use em-dashes, not bullets
103. - Blockquote left border is accent color
104. - The AI panel is labeled "Writing Companion", not "AI Chat"
105. - The caret/cursor color is --accent
106. - NEVER hardcode color values in components — always use CSS variables
107. - Semantic CSS variables in globals.css: `--success-text`, `--success-bg`, `--error-text`, `--error-bg`, `--error-bg-solid`, `--error-border`, `--text-inverse`, `--text-on-error`, `--font-ui`
108. - Run `node tests/css-audit.test.js` to catch any hardcoded colors
109. - Panels must be self-contained and position-agnostic
110. 
111. ## Design Measurements
112. - Activity rail width: 48px
113. - Rail icon size: 20×20, hit area 36×36
114. - Rail vertical gap: 4px between icons
115. - File node row height: 28px
116. - File tree indentation: 16px per depth level
117. - Chevron rotation: 90° expanded, 150ms ease
118. - Dropdown animation: 150ms fadeIn with -4px translateY
119. - Title bar height: 46px
120. - Status bar height: 32px
121. - Settings modal width: 480px, max-height 70vh
122. 
123. ## Known Issues
124. 
125. - DM Sans (UI font) is loaded from Google Fonts CDN — requires internet on first launch. Falls back to `-apple-system` / system sans-serif if offline. Consider bundling as woff2 for full offline support.
126. - Alternative editor/preview fonts (JetBrains Mono, Source Serif 4, Newsreader) also load from Google Fonts CDN. Default fonts (iA Writer Mono, Literata) are self-hosted and work offline.
127. - Version: 0.0.7
128. - Phase 1: Complete
129. - Phase 1.5: Activity Rail — Complete
130. - Phase 2: AI integration — Complete (Copilot SDK, slash commands, model picker)
131. - Phase 2.5: SDK Optimization — Complete (custom tools, file attachments, session persistence, abort, reasoning effort, infinite sessions, diff preview, interactive prompts)
132. - Phase 3: Keyboard shortcuts, file operations — Complete
133. - **v1 MVP: Complete**
134. 
135. ## Feedback Submission Configuration
136. 
137. - User-submitted feedback from the app is sent to `jayparikh/graphite-release` as GitHub issues.
138. - Build-time token variable: `VITE_GITHUB_FEEDBACK_TOKEN`
139. - Required scope: fine-grained PAT with **Issues: Read and write** on `jayparikh/graphite-release` only.
140. - The token is bundled into the Electron main-process build via `electron.vite.config.js`.
141. 
142. ## GitHub Copilot SDK Integration
143. 
144. ### Key Files
145. - `electron/ai-client.js` — Singleton wrapper for CopilotClient, manages sessions, streaming, custom tools
146. - `src/lib/command-registry.js` — Slash command definitions (/help, /continue, /improve, etc.)
147. - `src/hooks/useAISettings.js` — AI settings hook (auto-apply, diff preview, reasoning effort)
148. 
149. ### SDK Features Used (v0.1.29)
150. - **Custom tools** (`defineTool`): insert_text, replace_selection, append_text, get_document_stats, get_selection, search_document
151. - **Diff preview**: Tool edits routed through proposal flow with Accept/Reject UI (DiffView component)
152. - **Interactive prompts** (`onUserInputRequest`): SDK can ask user questions via inline prompt UI with choices/freeform
153. - **File attachments**: Native `attachments` in MessageOptions for file context
154. - **Session persistence**: `resumeSession()` + `getMessages()` for chat history across restarts
155. - **Abort**: `session.abort()` with stop button in UI; cleans up pending proposals and user inputs
156. - **System message append mode**: Keeps SDK guardrails + custom Writing Companion personality
157. - **Infinite sessions**: Automatic context compaction for long conversations
158. - **Reasoning effort**: Configurable via Settings (low/medium/high)
159. - **Client metadata**: `clientName: "Graphite"`, `workingDirectory` for context
160. 
161. ### Integration Learnings
162. 
163. **ESM vs CommonJS mismatch:**
164. The SDK (`@github/copilot-sdk`) is ESM-only, but Electron's main process uses CommonJS. Use dynamic import:
165. ```javascript
166. const sdk = await import('@github/copilot-sdk');
167. CopilotClient = sdk.CopilotClient;
168. ```
169. 
170. **Connection state matters:**
171. Can't call `listModels()` immediately after `start()` — the client needs time to reach `connected` state. Poll until ready:
172. ```javascript
173. while (client.getState() !== 'connected' && attempts < 50) {
174.   await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));
175. }
176. ```
177. 
178. **Initialization timing:**
179. The renderer requests models on mount, but the AI client may not be started yet. Ensure `start()` is called in the IPC handler before any SDK operations.
180. 
181. **Build configuration:**
182. The ai-client module must be added as a separate rollup input in `electron.vite.config.js` so it gets bundled correctly.
183. 
184. **Streaming architecture:**
185. The SDK uses event-based streaming (`session.on('assistant.message_delta')`). Forward chunks from main process via `webContents.send()` and listen in renderer with `ipcRenderer.on()`.
186. 
187. **Copilot CLI dependency:**
188. The SDK shells out to the Copilot CLI binary for auth and JSON-RPC. It auto-discovers the CLI from VS Code's installation path — users need VS Code with Copilot extension installed.
189. 
190. **Diff preview architecture:**
191. Tool edits flow through a proposal system: ai-client → main.js (proposeEdit callback) → renderer (ai:toolEditProposal IPC). The renderer captures the current document text AND cursor/selection positions at proposal time, builds the full diff preview (newText = full document with change spliced in), and stores everything in `pendingProposal`. On Accept, stored positions are used — not current cursor. Proposal IDs include random suffixes to prevent collisions. 120-second timeout auto-dismisses via `ai:proposalTimeout` event.
192. 
193. **User input request flow:**
194. `onUserInputRequest` handler creates a Promise that blocks the SDK until the user answers. Requests are tracked in `pendingUserInputs` Map with 60-second timeout. Timeout sends `ai:userInputTimeout` to renderer. Both `abort()` and `clearSession()` call `_clearPendingUserInputs()` to resolve all pending promises and prevent dangling UI.
195. 
196. **IPC security pattern:**
197. Preload bridge validates IPC channel names before forwarding — e.g., `replySearchResult` checks channel starts with `ai:searchResult-`, `replyProposalResult` prefixes with `ai:proposalResult-`. Never allow renderer to invoke arbitrary IPC channels.
198.

 # Graphite — Claude Code Configuration
 
 ## Project Overview
 Graphite is an Electron-based markdown writing IDE with a flexible panel system: file explorer, editor (CodeMirror 6), live preview, and AI writing companion. It features a theme system with paired dark/light themes and movable/resizable panels.
 
 ## Tech Stack
 - Electron + electron-vite
 - React 18
 - CodeMirror 6 (editor)
 - markdown-it (preview rendering)
 - GitHub Copilot SDK (@github/copilot-sdk) — AI chat with streaming
 - JSON-based theme system
 
 ## Design Principles
 - This is a WRITING tool, not a code editor. Every decision should favor the writing experience.
 - The aesthetic is "Linear meets Obsidian" — refined, quiet, precise.
 - The default theme is "Silver" (cool graphite, silvery accent #8b9bb5).
 - Typography: DM Sans for UI, Source Serif 4 for preview, JetBrains Mono for editor.
 - Motion should be subtle and purposeful. No decorative animations.
 - The app should feel like opening a beautiful notebook.
 - ALL colors must come from CSS variables set by the theme loader. Never hardcode colors.
 
 ## Panel System Architecture
 - Panels are self-contained, position-agnostic components
 - User can drag panels to reorder (left ↔ right)
 - User can resize panel widths via drag handles
 - Panel positions/sizes persist to user preferences
 - Terminal panel type is reserved for v2 (scaffolded but not implemented)
 
 ## Theme System
 - Themes are JSON files defining CSS variable values (see src/themes/built-in/)
 - 5 built-in themes: Silver, Slate, Ivory (dark) and Frost, Paper (light)
 - Single dropdown shows all themes grouped by Dark/Light category
 - Custom themes can be installed by dropping JSON into ~/.graphite/themes/
 - The CodeMirror theme in editor-theme.js MUST read from CSS variables, not hardcoded values
 - When adding any new UI element, always use CSS variables (--bg-primary, --accent, etc.)
 
 ## Code Style
 - Functional React components with hooks
 - CSS variables for ALL design tokens (set by theme loader at runtime)
 - No CSS-in-JS libraries — use CSS modules or plain CSS
 - File naming: PascalCase for components, camelCase for utilities
 - Keep components focused and small (< 150 lines ideally)
 
 ## Key Files
 - `electron/main.js` — Main process, window creation, IPC setup, AI handlers
 - `electron/preload.js` — Context bridge for renderer (includes theme IPC, menu events, AI API)
 - `electron/ai-client.js` — Copilot SDK wrapper, session management, streaming, custom tools
 - `src/App.jsx` — Root component, file loading, dirty state, save handling
 - `src/lib/command-registry.js` — Slash command definitions for AI chat
 - `src/lib/diff-utils.js` — Line-based LCS diff algorithm for AI edit previews
 - `src/themes/theme-loader.js` — Reads JSON themes, applies CSS variables to :root
 - `src/themes/theme-registry.js` — Discovers built-in + user themes, manages pairs
 - `src/hooks/useTheme.js` — Theme state, persistence
 - `src/hooks/useFileSystem.js` — File operations (read, write, directory listing)
 - `src/hooks/useAISettings.js` — AI settings hook (auto-apply, diff preview, reasoning effort)
 - `src/components/Editor/editor-theme.js` — CodeMirror theme (MUST use CSS variables)
 - `src/components/FileExplorer/FileNode.jsx` — Tree item with expand/collapse
 - `src/components/FileExplorer/FileSearch.jsx` — Search filtering
 - `src/components/ThemeSwitcher/ThemeSwitcher.jsx` — Theme dropdown (Dark/Light groups)
 - `src/components/Panels/PanelManager.jsx` — Context provider for panel state
 - `src/components/Panels/PanelContainer.jsx` — Resizable, reorderable layout
 - `src/components/ActivityRail/ActivityRail.jsx` — Left navigation rail
 - `src/components/ActivityRail/RailButton.jsx` — Individual rail icon button
 - `src/components/Settings/SettingsModal.jsx` — Settings dialog (includes Writing Companion section)
 - `src/components/AIChat/AIChat.jsx` — AI chat panel with streaming, diff preview, user input prompts
 - `src/components/AIChat/DiffView.jsx` — Unified diff preview component with Accept/Reject
 - `src/components/AIChat/DiffView.css` — Diff styling (uses theme semantic variables)
 - `src/styles/globals.css` — CSS variable declarations and resets
 
 ## Testing
 
 ### Automated Tests
 - `npm run test` — builds then runs all Playwright tests
 - `npx playwright test --project=functional` — runs functional tests only (requires prior build)
 - `npx playwright test --project=visual` — runs visual/layout tests only
 - `node tests/diff-utils.test.js` — runs diff algorithm unit tests (standalone Node)
 - `node tests/css-audit.test.js` — scans all CSS files for hardcoded color violations
 
 ### Test Files
 - `tests/app.spec.js` — basic app launch and UI element verification
 - `tests/functional.spec.js` — core workflow: file open, edit, save, panel toggles, theme switch
 - `tests/selection-toolbar.spec.js` — selection toolbar appear/disappear
 - `tests/feedback-modal.spec.js` — feedback modal open/close, validation states
 - `tests/settings-modal.spec.js` — settings modal, Writing Companion section (toggles, dropdown, persistence)
 - `tests/diff-utils.test.js` — unit tests for `computeLineDiff` and `diffSummary`
 - `tests/css-audit.test.js` — CSS variable discipline audit (flags hardcoded hex/rgb colors)
 - `tests/visual.spec.js`, `tests/layout-*.spec.js`, `tests/theme-parity.spec.js` — visual regression tests
 
 ### Manual QA Checklist
 - The editor should feel responsive with no lag on keystroke
 - Preview should update live as you type
 - File explorer should reflect actual filesystem
 - Switching themes should be instant (no flash, no reload)
 - All 5 built-in themes should look correct
 - Panel reordering and resizing should be smooth
 - Dirty indicator (●) should appear when file is modified
 
 ## Important Notes
 - No line numbers in the editor
 - Soft word wrap enabled
 - List items in preview use em-dashes, not bullets
 - Blockquote left border is accent color
 - The AI panel is labeled "Writing Companion", not "AI Chat"
 - The caret/cursor color is --accent
 - NEVER hardcode color values in components — always use CSS variables
 - Semantic CSS variables in globals.css: `--success-text`, `--success-bg`, `--error-text`, `--error-bg`, `--error-bg-solid`, `--error-border`, `--text-inverse`, `--text-on-error`, `--font-ui`
 - Run `node tests/css-audit.test.js` to catch any hardcoded colors
 - Panels must be self-contained and position-agnostic
 
 ## Design Measurements
 - Activity rail width: 48px
 - Rail icon size: 20×20, hit area 36×36
 - Rail vertical gap: 4px between icons
 - File node row height: 28px
 - File tree indentation: 16px per depth level
 - Chevron rotation: 90° expanded, 150ms ease
 - Dropdown animation: 150ms fadeIn with -4px translateY
 - Title bar height: 46px
 - Status bar height: 32px
 - Settings modal width: 480px, max-height 70vh
 
 ## Known Issues
 
 - DM Sans (UI font) is loaded from Google Fonts CDN — requires internet on first launch. Falls back to `-apple-system` / system sans-serif if offline. Consider bundling as woff2 for full offline support.
 - Alternative editor/preview fonts (JetBrains Mono, Source Serif 4, Newsreader) also load from Google Fonts CDN. Default fonts (iA Writer Mono, Literata) are self-hosted and work offline.
 - Version: 0.0.7
 - Phase 1: Complete
 - Phase 1.5: Activity Rail — Complete
 - Phase 2: AI integration — Complete (Copilot SDK, slash commands, model picker)
 - Phase 2.5: SDK Optimization — Complete (custom tools, file attachments, session persistence, abort, reasoning effort, infinite sessions, diff preview, interactive prompts)
 - Phase 3: Keyboard shortcuts, file operations — Complete
 - **v1 MVP: Complete**
 
 ## Feedback Submission Configuration
 
 - User-submitted feedback from the app is sent to `jayparikh/graphite-release` as GitHub issues.
 - Build-time token variable: `VITE_GITHUB_FEEDBACK_TOKEN`
 - Required scope: fine-grained PAT with **Issues: Read and write** on `jayparikh/graphite-release` only.
 - The token is bundled into the Electron main-process build via `electron.vite.config.js`.
 
 ## GitHub Copilot SDK Integration
 
 ### Key Files
 - `electron/ai-client.js` — Singleton wrapper for CopilotClient, manages sessions, streaming, custom tools
 - `src/lib/command-registry.js` — Slash command definitions (/help, /continue, /improve, etc.)
 - `src/hooks/useAISettings.js` — AI settings hook (auto-apply, diff preview, reasoning effort)
 
 ### SDK Features Used (v0.1.29)
 - **Custom tools** (`defineTool`): insert_text, replace_selection, append_text, get_document_stats, get_selection, search_document
 - **Diff preview**: Tool edits routed through proposal flow with Accept/Reject UI (DiffView component)
 - **Interactive prompts** (`onUserInputRequest`): SDK can ask user questions via inline prompt UI with choices/freeform
 - **File attachments**: Native `attachments` in MessageOptions for file context
 - **Session persistence**: `resumeSession()` + `getMessages()` for chat history across restarts
 - **Abort**: `session.abort()` with stop button in UI; cleans up pending proposals and user inputs
 - **System message append mode**: Keeps SDK guardrails + custom Writing Companion personality
 - **Infinite sessions**: Automatic context compaction for long conversations
 - **Reasoning effort**: Configurable via Settings (low/medium/high)
 - **Client metadata**: `clientName: "Graphite"`, `workingDirectory` for context
 
 ### Integration Learnings
 
 **ESM vs CommonJS mismatch:**
 The SDK (`@github/copilot-sdk`) is ESM-only, but Electron's main process uses CommonJS. Use dynamic import:
 ```javascript
 const sdk = await import('@github/copilot-sdk');
 CopilotClient = sdk.CopilotClient;
 ```
 
 **Connection state matters:**
 Can't call `listModels()` immediately after `start()` — the client needs time to reach `connected` state. Poll until ready:
 ```javascript
 while (client.getState() !== 'connected' && attempts < 50) {
   await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));
 }
 ```
 
 **Initialization timing:**
 The renderer requests models on mount, but the AI client may not be started yet. Ensure `start()` is called in the IPC handler before any SDK operations.
 
 **Build configuration:**
 The ai-client module must be added as a separate rollup input in `electron.vite.config.js` so it gets bundled correctly.
 
 **Streaming architecture:**
 The SDK uses event-based streaming (`session.on('assistant.message_delta')`). Forward chunks from main process via `webContents.send()` and listen in renderer with `ipcRenderer.on()`.
 
 **Copilot CLI dependency:**
 The SDK shells out to the Copilot CLI binary for auth and JSON-RPC. It auto-discovers the CLI from VS Code's installation path — users need VS Code with Copilot extension installed.
 
 **Diff preview architecture:**
 Tool edits flow through a proposal system: ai-client → main.js (proposeEdit callback) → renderer (ai:toolEditProposal IPC). The renderer captures the current document text AND cursor/selection positions at proposal time, builds the full diff preview (newText = full document with change spliced in), and stores everything in `pendingProposal`. On Accept, stored positions are used — not current cursor. Proposal IDs include random suffixes to prevent collisions. 120-second timeout auto-dismisses via `ai:proposalTimeout` event.
 
 **User input request flow:**
 `onUserInputRequest` handler creates a Promise that blocks the SDK until the user answers. Requests are tracked in `pendingUserInputs` Map with 60-second timeout. Timeout sends `ai:userInputTimeout` to renderer. Both `abort()` and `clearSession()` call `_clearPendingUserInputs()` to resolve all pending promises and prevent dangling UI.
 
 **IPC security pattern:**
 Preload bridge validates IPC channel names before forwarding — e.g., `replySearchResult` checks channel starts with `ai:searchResult-`, `replyProposalResult` prefixes with `ai:proposalResult-`. Never allow renderer to invoke arbitrary IPC channels.

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a summary:

## Summary: Graphite Roadmap & Planned Features

### **Current Version**
- **v0.0.7** (as of package.json)
- **v1 MVP: Complete** ✅

---

### **Completed Phases & Milestones**

| Phase | Status | Features |
|-------|--------|----------|
| **Phase 1** | ✅ Complete | Core app (editor, preview, file explorer, themes, panels) |
| **Phase 1.5** | ✅ Complete | Activity Rail navigation |
| **Phase 2** | ✅ Complete | AI integration (GitHub Copilot SDK, slash commands, model picker) |
| **Phase 2.5** | ✅ Complete | SDK Optimization (custom tools, file attachments, session persistence, diff preview, interactive prompts, reasoning effort) |
| **Phase 3** | ✅ Complete | Keyboard shortcuts, file operations, focus mode |
| **Phase 4** | ✅ Complete | Export (PDF/DOCX/HTML/plain text) |

---

### **Planned Future Features (Unimplemented)**

#### **Phase 5: Writing Statistics & Goals** (Planned)
- Session stats: words written today
- Daily word count goals with progress indicator
- Writing streak tracking
- Historical view (calendar heatmap)
- Per-document stats
- Status bar display or dedicated stats panel

#### **Phase 6: Version History / Local Snapshots** (Planned)
- Auto-save snapshots every 5 minutes
- Timeline view to browse previous versions
- Visual diff showing changes
- Restore any previous version (keep last 100 per document)
- Keyboard shortcut: `Cmd+Shift+V`

#### **Future Enhancements (Reserved):**
- **Writing Insights Dashboard**: Vocabulary richness, sentence length variation, Flesch-Kincaid reading level, word frequency visualization, overused words detection, passive voice percentage
- **Document Outline / Navigation Panel** ⚠️ (Needs refinement - was attempted in v0.0.4 but removed due to implementation issues; essential feature that competitors have)
- **Reading Mode**: Full-screen distraction-free reading, pagination, progress indicator
- **Integrated Terminal**: Panel for advanced filesystem & AI tasks (architecture scaffolded but not implemented)
- **Git Integration**: See file changes, commit from app
- **Theme Gallery**: Browse and install community themes
- **Visual Theme Editor**: Create custom themes in-app with live preview
- **Plugin System**: Extend markdown rendering
- **Multiple Tabs/Buffers**
- **System color scheme auto-detection** (follow OS dark/light preference)

---

### **Known Issues & Gaps (from Design Review)**

#### **Critical Issues:**
1. **Typing indicator conflict** — `.typing-indicator` defined in both animations.css and AIChat.css with different layouts, causing message height/alignment shifts
2. **CodeMirror dark theme bug** — Always flagged as dark even in light themes (Frost/Paper), causing incorrect contrast and styling

#### **High Priority Issues:**
1. **Title bar brand offset** — Hard-coded at 80px for macOS traffic lights; breaks alignment on non-macOS or when traffic lights are moved
2. **Preview column misalignment** — Left-aligned with fixed max-width while editor is centered, creating visual jump when toggling preview
3. **Dropdown overflow** — Theme switcher and AI model dropdown lack viewport clamping, can render off-screen
4. **Light theme styling issues** — Light themes inherit dark-theme defaults for selection/tooltip contrast

#### **Medium Priority Issues:**
1. **Padding misalignment** — AI Chat message list (16px) vs input container (12px margin) causes vertical wobble
2. **File Explorer alignment** — Search row (12px) vs panel headers (16px) padding mismatch
3. **File rename styling** — Uses `bg-primary` in `bg-panel` context, reads like a cutout rather than integrated edit state
4. **Activity Rail indicator collision** — Sits 6px outside rail width; on right rail, collides with panel border
5. **Status bar path width** — Fixed at 400px, causes crowding on small windows or unused space on large windows

#### **Low Priority Issues:**
1. **Font weight inconsistency** — Context menu font-weight: 450 not supported on all platforms (renders as 400 or 500)
2. **Preview typography contrast** — h4-h6 collapse into body size, making long documents feel flat
3. **Chevron rotation redundancy** — Two separate rotation systems can cause visual drift

---

### **Known External Dependencies & Limitations**

- **Fonts require internet**: DM Sans (UI), JetBrains Mono, Source Serif 4, Newsreader load from Google Fonts CDN. iA Writer Mono and Literata are self-hosted
- **Copilot dependency**: Users need VS Code with GitHub Copilot extension installed and `copilot auth login` run in terminal
- **Apple Design Awards**: Requires native SwiftUI rewrite (Electron apps have never won — Apple rewards native platform tech)

---

### **No TODO/FIXME Comments Found**
Grep search for TODO, FIXME, HACK comments in source code returned no results.

1. **Font weight inconsistency** — Context menu font-weight: 450 not supported on all platforms (renders as 400 or 500)
2. **Preview typography contrast** — h4-h6 collapse into body size, making long documents feel flat
3. **Chevron rotation redundancy** — Two separate rotation systems can cause visual drift

---

### **Known External Dependencies & Limitations**

- **Fonts require internet**: DM Sans (UI), JetBrains Mono, Source Serif 4, Newsreader load from Google Fonts CDN. iA Writer Mono and Literata are self-hosted
- **Copilot dependency**: Users need VS Code with GitHub Copilot extension installed and `copilot auth login` run in terminal
- **Apple Design Awards**: Requires native SwiftUI rewrite (Electron apps have never won — Apple rewards native platform tech)

---

### **No TODO/FIXME Comments Found**
Grep search for TODO, FIXME, HACK comments in source code returned no results.

## Summary: Graphite Roadmap & Planned Features

### **Current Version**

---

### **Completed Phases & Milestones**

| Phase | Status | Features |
|-------|--------|----------|

| **Phase 1.5** | ✅ Complete | Activity Rail navigation |
| **Phase 2** | ✅ Complete | AI integration (GitHub Copilot SDK, slash commands, model picker) |
| **Phase 2.5** | ✅ Complete | SDK Optimization (custom tools, file attachments, session persistence, diff preview, interactive prompts, reasoning effort) |
| **Phase 3** | ✅ Complete | Keyboard shortcuts, file operations, focus mode |
| **Phase 4** | ✅ Complete | Export (PDF/DOCX/HTML/plain text) |

---

### **Planned Future Features (Unimplemented)**

#### **Phase 5: Writing Statistics & Goals** (Planned)
- Session stats: words written today
- Daily word count goals with progress indicator
- Writing streak tracking
- Historical view (calendar heatmap)
- Per-document stats
- Status bar display or dedicated stats panel

#### **Phase 6: Version History / Local Snapshots** (Planned)
- Auto-save snapshots every 5 minutes
- Timeline view to browse previous versions
- Visual diff showing changes
- Restore any previous version (keep last 100 per document)
- Keyboard shortcut: `Cmd+Shift+V`

#### **Future Enhancements (Reserved):**
- **Writing Insights Dashboard**: Vocabulary richness, sentence length variation, Flesch-Kincaid reading level, word frequency visualization, overused words detection, passive voice percentage
- **Document Outline / Navigation Panel** ⚠️ (Needs refinement - was attempted in v0.0.4 but removed due to implementation issues; essential feature that competitors have)
- **Reading Mode**: Full-screen distraction-free reading, pagination, progress indicator
- **Integrated Terminal**: Panel for advanced filesystem & AI tasks (architecture scaffolded but not implemented)
- **Git Integration**: See file changes, commit from app
- **Theme Gallery**: Browse and install community themes
- **Visual Theme Editor**: Create custom themes in-app with live preview
- **Plugin System**: Extend markdown rendering
- **Multiple Tabs/Buffers**
- **System color scheme auto-detection** (follow OS dark/light preference)

---

### **Known Issues & Gaps (from Design Review)**

#### **Critical Issues:**

3. **Dropdown overflow** — Theme switcher and AI model dropdown lack viewport clamping, can render off-screen
4. **Light theme styling issues** — Light themes inherit dark-theme defaults for selection/tooltip contrast

#### **Medium Priority Issues:**
1. **Padding misalignment** — AI Chat message list (16px) vs input container (12px margin) causes vertical wobble
2. **File Explorer alignment** — Search row (12px) vs panel headers (16px) padding mismatch
3. **File rename styling** — Uses `bg-primary` in `bg-panel` context, reads like a cutout rather than integrated edit state
4. **Activity Rail indicator collision** — Sits 6px outside rail width; on right rail, collides with panel border
5. **Status bar path width** — Fixed at 400px, causes crowding on small windows or unused space on large windows

#### **Low Priority Issues:**
1. **Font weight inconsistency** — Context menu font-weight: 450 not supported on all platforms (renders as 400 or 500)
2. **Preview typography contrast** — h4-h6 collapse into body size, making long documents feel flat
3. **Chevron rotation redundancy** — Two separate rotation systems can cause visual drift

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. # Graphite Design Review (2026-02-04)
2. 
3. Scope: UI composition, spacing, typography, interaction states, and visual consistency based on current code. This is a static review (no runtime screenshots).
4. 
5. ## What Works Well
6. - Cohesive graphite palette with subtle chroma and low-contrast surfaces that keep focus on content.
7. - Clear hierarchy between app chrome (title/status bars), rails, panels, and editor surfaces.
8. - Editor and preview padding symmetry (32px/40px) gives a stable reading rhythm.
9. - Consistent micro-radius language (4px-10px) and soft transitions across controls.
10. - Context menu styling is premium and feels intentional (blur, inset highlight, shadow stack).
11. - Typographic cadence in preview (serif, 1.75 line-height, careful heading spacing) reads well.
12. 
13. ## Issues / Bugs / Pixel-Polish Gaps
14. 
15. ### Critical
16. - Typing indicator styles conflict: .typing-indicator is defined in both animations.css and AIChat.css with different layouts and padding, so the chat bubble can pick up unintended padding and flex rules depending on cascade order. This can visibly shift message height and alignment.
17. 
18. ### High
19. - Title bar brand offset is hard-coded at 80px for macOS traffic lights. On non-macOS or when traffic lights are moved (or hidden), the brand is still offset and the wordmark appears visually off-center. This breaks “pixel perfect” alignment across platforms.
20. - CodeMirror theme is always flagged as dark (dark: true) even when light themes are active. This causes light themes to inherit dark-theme defaults (selection/tooltip contrast and editor shadowing), producing inconsistent contrast in Frost/Paper.
21. - Preview column is left-aligned with fixed max-width but no centering, while the editor is full-width with equal padding. This creates a lateral jump when toggling preview and visually unbalances the layout; the reading column looks “stuck to the left.”
22. - Theme switcher dropdown has no viewport clamping, so it can overflow off-screen near the right/top edges on narrow windows. ContextMenu handles this, but ThemeSwitcher doesn’t.
23. - AI model dropdown also lacks viewport clamping, so it can render off-screen (especially when the input is near the bottom of the panel). This is a common visual defect in compact windows.
24. 
25. ### Medium
26. - AI Chat message list uses 16px padding but the input container has a 12px margin, causing the input box to not align with message content. This is a visible “wobble” in the vertical column.
27. - File Explorer search row uses 12px horizontal padding, while panel headers use 16px, creating a slight misalignment between the header actions and the search field text.
28. - File rename input uses bg-primary in a bg-panel context. It reads like a cutout rather than an integrated edit state; the input is darker than the list row and looks off-theme.
29. - Activity Rail active indicator sits 6px outside the rail width. On the right rail, the indicator can visually collide with the panel border, creating a double-stroke effect.
30. - Status bar path max width is fixed at 400px; on small windows it can visually crowd left stats, while on large windows it leaves unused space. The balance shifts with window size.
31. 
32. ### Low
33. - Context menu item font-weight is 450. Some font files don’t include a 450 weight, so this can render as 400 or 500 depending on platform, causing subtle cross-OS inconsistency.
34. - Typography heading sizes in preview (h1 28px, h2 22px, h3 18px, h4 15.5px) are close to body size. h4-h6 collapse into body size, making long documents feel flat. Consider slightly more contrast or weight variation.
35. - File explorer chevron rotation uses two separate systems (.file-node-chevron.expanded and .chevron.open in animations.css). The redundancy can cause visual drift if one class is used elsewhere.
36. 
37. ## Overall Impression
38. Graphite already reads as a thoughtful, premium writing tool. The palette and typography choices are strong and consistent. The most visible polish wins are tightening alignment between column edges and ensuring light themes truly render as light. Fixing the dropdown overflow and the typing indicator conflict will also remove the most noticeable “off by a few pixels” moments.
39.

data.content:
Here's the breakdown:

### Critical
- **Typing indicator conflict** — `.typing-indicator` defined in both `animations.css` and `AIChat.css` with different layouts. Depending on CSS cascade order, the chat bubble gets wrong padding/flex, causing message height to jump.

### High
- **Title bar offset** — Brand wordmark is hard-coded 80px left for macOS traffic lights. Looks off-center on any other platform or if traffic lights move.
- **CodeMirror always "dark"** — `dark: true` is set even for light themes (Frost/Paper), so the editor inherits dark-mode defaults for selection, tooltips, and shadows. Light themes don't look truly light.
- **Preview off-center** — Preview column is left-aligned with fixed max-width while editor is centered, creating a visual jump when toggling.
- **Dropdown overflow** — Theme switcher and AI model picker can render off-screen on narrow/small windows. Context menu handles this, but dropdowns don't.

### Medium
- **AI Chat padding wobble** — Message list (16px) vs input container (12px) misalignment.
- **File Explorer padding mismatch** — Search row (12px) vs panel headers (16px).
- **Rename input looks like a cutout** — Uses `bg-primary` in a `bg-panel` context.
- **Rail indicator collision** — Active indicator extends 6px outside the rail, can overlap panel border.
- **Status bar path width** — Fixed 400px, doesn't adapt to window size.

### Low
- Font weight 450 inconsistency, heading size flattening, redundant chevron rotation systems.

The highest-impact fixes would be the **CodeMirror light theme bug** and **typing indicator conflict** — those are the most user-visible. Want to tackle some of these?
I think we should fix all of these. double check that these are legit first.

1. /**
2.  * Graphite — CodeMirror 6 Theme
3.  * 
4.  * Custom theme that reads from CSS variables so it automatically
5.  * updates when the app theme changes. No hardcoded colors.
6.  * 
7.  * Per spec:
8.  * - Font: JetBrains Mono, 14px, line-height 1.8
9.  * - Caret: --accent color
10.  * - No line numbers
11.  * - Soft word wrap
12.  */
13. 
14. import { EditorView } from '@codemirror/view';
15. 
16. /**
17.  * Get a CSS variable value from the document root
18.  */
19. function cssVar(name) {
20.   return getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue(name).trim();
21. }
22. 
23. /**
24.  * Create the Graphite editor theme
25.  * This returns a fresh theme extension that reads current CSS variable values
26.  */
27. export function createGraphiteTheme(isDark = true) {
28.   return EditorView.theme({
29.     // Root editor styles
30.     '&': {
31.       height: '100%',
32.       fontSize: 'var(--font-editor-size)',
33.       backgroundColor: 'var(--bg-editor)',
34.     },
35. 
36.     // Scroller (contains the content)
37.     '.cm-scroller': {
38.       fontFamily: 'var(--font-editor)',
39.       lineHeight: '1.8',
40.       padding: '32px 40px',
41.       overflow: 'auto',
42.     },
43. 
44.     // Content area
45.     '.cm-content': {
46.       caretColor: 'var(--accent)',
47.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
48.       padding: '0',
49.     },
50. 
51.     // All lines - ensure no underlines by default
52.     '.cm-line': {
53.       padding: '0',
54.       textDecoration: 'none',
55.     },
56. 
57.     // Ensure no underlines on any span inside lines
58.     '.cm-line span': {
59.       textDecoration: 'none',
60.     },
61. 
62.     // Cursor
63.     '.cm-cursor, .cm-dropCursor': {
64.       borderLeftColor: 'var(--accent)',
65.       borderLeftWidth: '2px',
66.     },
67. 
68.     // Selection
69.     '&.cm-focused .cm-selectionBackground, .cm-selectionBackground': {
70.       backgroundColor: 'var(--accent-dim)',
71.     },
72. 
73.     '.cm-selectionMatch': {
74.       backgroundColor: 'var(--accent-dim)',
75.     },
76. 
77.     // Active line highlight (subtle)
78.     '.cm-activeLine': {
79.       backgroundColor: 'transparent',
80.     },
81. 
82.     // Gutters (hidden - no line numbers)
83.     '.cm-gutters': {
84.       display: 'none',
85.     },
86. 
87.     // Placeholder text
88.     '.cm-placeholder': {
89.       color: 'var(--text-tertiary)',
90.       fontStyle: 'normal',
91.     },
92. 
93.     // Focus outline
94.     '&.cm-focused': {
95.       outline: 'none',
96.     },
97. 
98.     // Markdown-specific syntax highlighting
99.     '.cm-header': {
100.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
101.       fontWeight: '600',
102.       textDecoration: 'none',
103.     },
104. 
105.     '.cm-header-1': {
106.       fontSize: '1.5em',
107.     },
108. 
109.     '.cm-header-2': {
110.       fontSize: '1.3em',
111.     },
112. 
113.     '.cm-header-3': {
114.       fontSize: '1.15em',
115.     },
116. 
117.     // Header markers (the # symbols)
118.     '.cm-heading-marker': {
119.       color: 'var(--text-tertiary)',
120.       fontWeight: '400',
121.     },
122. 
123.     '.cm-strong': {
124.       fontWeight: '600',
125.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
126.     },
127. 
128.     '.cm-emphasis': {
129.       fontStyle: 'italic',
130.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
131.     },
132. 
133.     '.cm-strikethrough': {
134.       textDecoration: 'line-through',
135.       color: 'var(--text-secondary)',
136.     },
137. 
138.     '.cm-link': {
139.       color: 'var(--accent)',
140.       textDecoration: 'none',
141.     },
142. 
143.     '.cm-url': {
144.       color: 'var(--text-tertiary)',
145.     },
146. 
147.     '.cm-code': {
148.       fontFamily: 'var(--font-mono)',
149.       backgroundColor: 'var(--hover)',
150.       borderRadius: '3px',
151.       padding: '1px 4px',
152.     },
153. 
154.     '.cm-quote': {
155.       color: 'var(--text-secondary)',
156.       fontStyle: 'italic',
157.     },
158. 
159.     '.cm-list': {
160.       color: 'var(--accent)',
161.     },
162. 
163.     '.cm-hr': {
164.       color: 'var(--border)',
165.     },
166. 
167.     '.cm-image': {
168.       color: 'var(--accent)',
169.     },
170. 
171.     // Search matches
172.     '.cm-searchMatch': {
173.       backgroundColor: 'var(--accent-dim)',
174.       outline: '1px solid var(--accent)',
175.     },
176. 
177.     '.cm-searchMatch.cm-searchMatch-selected': {
178.       backgroundColor: 'var(--accent)',
179.     },
180. 
181.     // Tooltips
182.     '.cm-tooltip': {
183.       backgroundColor: 'var(--bg-secondary)',
184.       border: '1px solid var(--border)',
185.       borderRadius: '6px',
186.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
187.     },
188. 
189.     // Panels (like search panel)
190.     '.cm-panels': {
191.       backgroundColor: 'var(--bg-panel)',
192.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
193.     },
194. 
195.     '.cm-panel': {
196.       padding: '8px 12px',
197.     },
198. 
199.     '.cm-panel input': {
200.       backgroundColor: 'var(--bg-secondary)',
201.       border: '1px solid var(--border)',
202.       borderRadius: '4px',
203.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
204.       padding: '4px 8px',
205.     },
206. 
207.     '.cm-panel button': {
208.       backgroundColor: 'var(--bg-secondary)',
209.       border: '1px solid var(--border)',
210.       borderRadius: '4px',
211.       color: 'var(--text-primary)',
212.       padding: '4px 12px',
213.       cursor: 'pointer',
214.     },
215. 
216.     '.cm-panel button:hover': {
217.       backgroundColor: 'var(--hover)',
218.     },
219.   }, { dark: isDark });
220. }
221. 
222. /**
223.  * Base theme configuration (non-color styles)
224.  */
225. export const graphiteBaseTheme = EditorView.baseTheme({
226.   '&': {

data.content:
Perfect! Now I have all the context. Let me compile the full answer for all 4 issues:

---

## **Issue 2: Title Bar 80px Offset**

**Full TitleBar Component (JSX):**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/TitleBar/TitleBar.jsx` (lines 1-65)
- Structure: Brand (logo + "GRAPHITE" wordmark) on left | Right toggles (Preview, Companion), theme switcher, filename

**Full TitleBar CSS:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/TitleBar/TitleBar.css` (lines 1-116)
- **Key offset:** Line 24 - `.title-bar-brand` has `padding-left: 80px;` with comment "Space for macOS traffic lights + breathing room"

**Is this Electron?** YES. This is an Electron app with OS detection:
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/electron/main.js`
- Platform checks at lines: **8, 34-37, 604, 969**
- Line 8: `if (process.platform === 'darwin')` — detects macOS
- Line 34-37: `getPlatformName()` function returns 'macOS', 'Windows', or 'Linux'
- macOS detection is used for app name setup and (presumably) for menu/window chrome handling

**What macOS Traffic Lights Need:**
- Standard macOS window controls (red/yellow/green buttons) are approximately **70px wide** (accounting for gaps)
- The 80px padding provides that space PLUS 10px breathing room
- **Problem:** On Windows/Linux, this 80px is wasted space since those OS handle window controls differently
- **Suggestion:** This should be conditional: apply 80px padding only on macOS, reduce it on Windows/Linux

---

## **Issue 8: File Explorer Padding Mismatch**

**FileExplorer.css `.panel-header` override:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/FileExplorer/FileExplorer.css` (lines 10-18)
- Line 10-13: `.file-explorer .panel-header` with `gap: 6px;` and **`padding-right: 12px;`** (override)
- Line 15-18: Compact variant with `gap: 4px;` and `padding-right: 8px;`

**Base PanelHeader CSS:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/Panels/PanelHeader.css` (lines 5-16)
- Line 5-16: `.panel-header` base has `gap: 8px;` and `padding: 0 16px;` (16px on both sides)

**File Search section:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/FileExplorer/FileExplorer.css` (lines 56-96)
- Line 56-63: `.file-search` has `padding: 8px 16px;` (consistent with base padding)

**Rationale for 12px override:**
- The 12px override reduces right padding to **make room for action buttons** (.panel-action buttons at lines 34-53)
- These buttons are 22px wide with flex layout, and the tighter padding prevents the header from becoming too cramped
- On compact mode, it goes even tighter: 8px padding + 4px gaps to maximize readable space

---

## **Issue 11: Status Bar Path Width**

**Full StatusBar CSS:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/StatusBar/StatusBar.css` (lines 1-63)

**Path width constraint:**
- Line 48-57: `.status-path` has **`max-width: 50vw;`** with overflow hidden and text-overflow ellipsis

**What's competing for space:**
- **Left side (`.status-bar-left`, lines 24-26):**
  - Word count (flex-shrink: 0)
  - Separator (·)
  - Char count (flex-shrink: 0)
  - Separator (·)
  - Read time estimate (flex-shrink: 0)
  - Optional focus mode label (flex-shrink: 0)
- **Right side (`.status-bar-right`, lines 28-33):**
  - File path (`.status-path` with the 50vw constraint)

**Analysis:**
- At 1440px: 50vw = 720px (generous, but needs to account for left stats)
- At 800px: 50vw = 400px (tight, but still has space)
- **Real issue:** Left stats are not shrinking (`flex-shrink: 0`), so on narrow windows, the path can get squeezed below 50vw
- The 50vw is more of a upper bound; actual width depends on left content occupying space first

---

## **Issue 14: Chevron Rotation Redundancy**

**FileExplorer.css chevron definition:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/components/FileExplorer/FileExplorer.css` (lines 142-155)
- Line 142-151: `.file-node-chevron` with base transition
- Line 153-155: `.file-node-chevron.expanded` with **`transform: rotate(90deg);`**

**animations.css chevron definition:**
- **File:** `/Users/jay/workspace/graphite/src/styles/animations.css` (lines 45-52)
- Line 46-48: `.chevron` base transition
- Line 50-52: `.chevron.open` with **`transform: rotate(90deg);`**

**Usage Analysis — `.chevron.open` is UNUSED:**

Actual chevron usage in code:
1. **File Explorer** → uses `.file-node-chevron` + `.expanded` class (lines 146 in FileNode.jsx)
2. **Theme Switcher** → uses `.theme-switcher-chevron` + `.open` class (line 142 in ThemeSwitcher.jsx)

The `.chevron.open` generic class in animations.css is **never applied** anywhere.

**Recommendation:**
- The generic `.chevron` and `.chevron.open` classes in animations.css can be **safely removed**
- Each component uses its own specific chevron class:
  - FileExplorer: `.file-node-chevron.expanded`
  - ThemeSwitcher: `.theme-switcher-chevron.open`
- No shared chevron rotation logic is needed

data.content: After the deep dive, I need to be honest — most of these aren't worth fixing:
data.reasoningText:
Now I have full context on all 4 issues. Let me assess which are truly worth fixing:

1. **Issue 2 (Title bar 80px offset)** - LEGIT. Should be conditional based on platform. But wait - this is an Electron app that only builds for macOS (the release workflow runs on `macos-latest`). Let me check if there are Windows/Linux builds too. Looking at the release workflow, it seems to only target macOS. If it's macOS-only, the 80px is correct and not an issue.

Actually, let me reconsider. The issue says "On non-macOS or when traffic lights are moved (or hidden)". 

Since the app is Electron and could theoretically run on other platforms, it's still a valid concern from a code perspective, but practically speaking, if it's only distributed as a macOS app, this is very low priority.

2. **Issue 8 (File Explorer padding)** - The override from 16px to 12px on the right side is intentional to make room for action buttons. This is a deliberate design choice, not a bug. The search row has 16px on both sides which matches the base panel header. The only mismatch is the panel header's right padding being reduced for button space, which seems like a reasonable design choice.

3. **Issue 11 (Status bar path width)** - Already uses 50vw, not a fixed 400px, so it's responsive. The concern about it being "too aggressive" is subjective.

4. **Issue 14 (Chevron rotation redundancy)** - The generic `.chevron.open` in animations.css is never used, so it's safe to remove.

Really, only issues 2 and 14 are worth addressing, and even 2 is questionable if this is macOS-only. Let me check the release workflow to see if there are Windows or Linux builds I should be considering.

|---|-------|-------|-----|
| **2** | Title bar 80px | **No** — macOS-only app, 80px is correct for traffic lights + breathing room |
| **8** | Explorer padding 12px | **No** — intentional override to fit action buttons in the header |
| **11** | Status bar 50vw | **No** — already responsive (50vw, not fixed 400px). Works fine across window sizes |
| **14** | Chevron redundancy | **Yes** — `.chevron` / `.chevron.open` in animations.css is dead code, never used |

Only **#14** is a genuine cleanup — unused CSS in animations.css. The rest are either intentional design decisions or already correct for a macOS-only app.

Want me to clean up the dead chevron CSS, or would you rather move on to something more impactful like Phase 5 (writing stats)?

arguments.file_text:
# Copilot SDK Upgrade: v0.1.29 → v0.1.32

## What Changed

Protocol version bumped from 2 → 3. The SDK now supports protocol version range negotiation (v2-v3) instead of exact matching.

### New Capabilities

**1. `session.setModel(modelId)` — In-session model switching**
Previously, the model was locked at session creation. Now you can switch models mid-conversation without destroying the session. Conversation history is preserved.

- **Impact on Graphite:** Currently if a user changes models in the AI dropdown, the next message still uses whatever model the session was created with (the model param in `getOrCreateSession` is only used at creation time, ignored for existing sessions). With `setModel()`, model changes take effect immediately.

**2. `session.disconnect()` replaces `session.destroy()`**
`destroy()` is deprecated (still works, forwards to `disconnect()`). Semantics clarified: `disconnect()` releases in-memory resources but preserves session data on disk. `deleteSession()` is for permanent removal.

- **Impact on Graphite:** Our `clearSession()` and `stop()` call `session.destroy()`. Should migrate to `disconnect()` to avoid deprecation warnings.

**3. `overridesBuiltInTool` flag for custom tools**
Tools can now explicitly declare they override a built-in tool of the same name. Without the flag, name clashes produce an error.

- **Impact on Graphite:** Informational — none of our tools clash with built-ins currently.

**4. `Symbol.asyncDispose` support**
Sessions support `await using session = ...` for automatic cleanup.

- **Impact on Graphite:** Nice-to-have, not critical for our usage pattern.

**5. Broadcast event handling (v3 protocol)**
Sessions now handle tool calls and permission requests via broadcast events with `requestId`-based response flow, in addition to the v2 synchronous RPC pattern.

- **Impact on Graphite:** Handled internally by the SDK. No code changes needed.

**6. `isChildProcess` mode**
SDK can run as a child of an existing Copilot CLI server process. Irrelevant for Graphite's use case.

## Implementation Plan

### Todo 1: Upgrade SDK dependency
Update `@github/copilot-sdk` from `0.1.29` to `0.1.32` in package.json.

### Todo 2: Migrate `destroy()` → `disconnect()`
Replace all `session.destroy()` calls with `session.disconnect()` in `electron/ai-client.js`.

### Todo 3: Implement in-session model switching
Update `sendMessage()` / `sendMessageSync()` to call `session.setModel(model)` when the requested model differs from the session's current model, instead of ignoring the model param for existing sessions.

This is the big UX win — users can switch models mid-conversation and it takes effect immediately with full history preserved.

### Todo 4: Build, test, verify
Run build + functional tests to confirm nothing breaks.

+**2. `session.disconnect()` replaces `session.destroy()`**
+`destroy()` is deprecated (still works, forwards to `disconnect()`). Semantics clarified: `disconnect()` releases in-memory resources but preserves session data on disk. `deleteSession()` is for permanent removal.
+
+- **Impact on Graphite:** Our `clearSession()` and `stop()` call `session.destroy()`. Should migrate to `disconnect()` to avoid deprecation warnings.
+
+**3. `overridesBuiltInTool` flag for custom tools**
+Tools can now explicitly declare they override a built-in tool of the same name. Without the flag, name clashes produce an error.
+
+- **Impact on Graphite:** Informational — none of our tools clash with built-ins currently.
+
+**4. `Symbol.asyncDispose` support**
+Sessions support `await using session = ...` for automatic cleanup.
+
+- **Impact on Graphite:** Nice-to-have, not critical for our usage pattern.
+
+**5. Broadcast event handling (v3 protocol)**
+Sessions now handle tool calls and permission requests via broadcast events with `requestId`-based response flow, in addition to the v2 synchronous RPC pattern.
+
+- **Impact on Graphite:** Handled internally by the SDK. No code changes needed.
+
+**6. `isChildProcess` mode**
+SDK can run as a child of an existing Copilot CLI server process. Irrelevant for Graphite's use case.
+
+## Implementation Plan
+
+### Todo 1: Upgrade SDK dependency
+Update `@github/copilot-sdk` from `0.1.29` to `0.1.32` in package.json.
+
+### Todo 2: Migrate `destroy()` → `disconnect()`
+Replace all `session.destroy()` calls with `session.disconnect()` in `electron/ai-client.js`.
+
+### Todo 3: Implement in-session model switching
+Update `sendMessage()` / `sendMessageSync()` to call `session.setModel(model)` when the requested model differs from the session's current model, instead of ignoring the model param for existing sessions.
+
+This is the big UX win — users can switch models mid-conversation and it takes effect immediately with full history preserved.
+
+### Todo 4: Build, test, verify
+Run build + functional tests to confirm nothing breaks.
+

data.content:
Here's what's new in SDK **v0.1.32** (up from our v0.1.29):

### Key new capabilities:

1. **`session.setModel(modelId)`** — Switch models mid-conversation without losing history. This is the big win — currently Graphite ignores model changes for existing sessions. With this, users can swap models and it takes effect immediately.

2. **`destroy()` → `disconnect()`** — `destroy()` is deprecated. Same behavior, just needs renaming.

3. **`overridesBuiltInTool` flag** — Tools can explicitly override built-ins. Not needed yet but good to know.

4. **Protocol v3** — Broadcast event handling for tools/permissions. Handled internally, no code changes needed.

### Plan:
1. Upgrade dependency (`0.1.29` → `0.1.32`)
2. Migrate `destroy()` → `disconnect()` (2 call sites)
3. Wire up `session.setModel()` for live model switching
4. Build + test

Want me to go ahead and implement?
yes

data.content: The RailButton uses `aria-label={label}` not `title`. The test selector `button[title="Settings"]` is wrong. Fix the test:

  const settingsBtn = window.locator('button[aria-label="Settings"]');

  const settingsBtn = window.locator('button[title="Settings"]');

data.content:
Here are my top 3 picks, ranked by "wow factor":

**🔥 Writing Statistics & Goals (Phase 5)** — This is the habit-forming feature. Daily word count tracking, writing streaks, and a GitHub-style calendar heatmap showing your writing history. Imagine opening Graphite and seeing a beautiful green grid of your writing consistency. It's the feature that makes writers *come back every day*.

**⏪ Version History / Local Snapshots (Phase 6)** — Auto-snapshot every few minutes, then a timeline scrubber to travel back through your document's evolution. It's a safety net that lets writers be bold — delete that paragraph, you can always get it back. Very satisfying UX when done right.

**📑 Document Outline Panel** — Essential for long-form writing. A live sidebar showing your heading structure that you can click to navigate. Was attempted in v0.0.4 but removed. Every competitor has it, and it's the missing piece for anyone writing anything longer than a blog post.

My vote: **Writing Statistics & Goals**. It's the most visually distinctive, it's what separates Graphite from "just another markdown editor," and a beautiful heatmap is the kind of thing people screenshot and share. What do you think?
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking me what feature I think would be awesome to work on next. Let me think about what would be most impactful and exciting for a writing IDE like Graphite.

From the roadmap:
- Phase 5: Writing Statistics & Goals (word count, streaks, calendar heatmap)
- Phase 6: Version History / Local Snapshots
- Document Outline / Navigation Panel
- Reading Mode
- Git Integration
- Theme Gallery / Visual Theme Editor
- Plugin System
- Multiple Tabs/Buffers

Let me think about what would be most exciting and impactful...

For a writing tool aimed at being award-level quality, I think the most "super awesome" features would be:


Writing Statistics & Goals stands out as the habit-forming feature that could become Graphite's signature—a beautiful calendar heatmap like GitHub's contribution graph would keep writers coming back daily. Document Outline is table stakes for long-form writing and competitors already have it. Version History would give writers that magical safety net to explore their document's evolution over time, and Reading Mode would round out the core experience.

I'm leaning toward presenting these options with genuine enthusiasm and letting the user decide which resonates most, though the writing stats feature feels like the real standout—it's visual, satisfying, and genuinely habit-forming.
